message.content:
Pesquise a documentação oficial do Blueprint.js v6 (blueprintjs.com) para entender:

1. **Theming e Dark Mode**: Como o dark mode funciona nativamente? Qual classe usar? Como aplicar?
2. **CSS Variables**: O Blueprint.js usa CSS variables próprias? Quais são?
3. **Best Practices**: Qual é a abordagem recomendada para styling? Devemos customizar ou usar os defaults?
4. **Typography e Colors**: O Blueprint tem seu próprio sistema de cores e tipografia?
5. **Layout e Spacing**: Existem classes utilitárias ou sistema de spacing?
6. **Components**: Como os componentes devem ser estilizados? Inline styles ou classes?

Use WebSearch e WebFetch para acessar:
- https://blueprintjs.com/docs/
- https://blueprintjs.com/docs/#core/colors
- https://blueprintjs.com/docs/#core/typography
- https://blueprintjs.com/docs/#core/classes

Retorne um resumo completo das melhores práticas do Blueprint.js para theming e styling.

content.text: Vou pesquisar a documentação oficial do Blueprint.js v6 para você.

input.prompt: Blueprint.js v6 documentation overview, theming, dark mode, styling
Blueprint.js color system, CSS variables, color palette
Blueprint.js typography, font system, text styling
Blueprint.js CSS classes, utility classes, spacing system

input.query: Blueprint.js v6 dark mode theming CSS variables 2025
Blueprint.js v6 styling best practices customization 2025

- Color system and palette management
- CSS variables for styling
- Design tokens and theming

However, the actual content detailing these features isn't included in the provided excerpt. For comprehensive information about Blueprint's color implementation, CSS variable usage, and color palette specifications, you would need to access the full documentation pages on the Blueprint.js website.

Based on my search, here's what I found about Blueprint.js dark mode theming and CSS variables:

## Blueprint.js Dark Mode Theming with CSS Variables

### Applying Dark Theme

Toggling themes involves applying the `bp4-dark` class to a parent element, which cascades updated CSS variables to child components.(no content)Utility classes like `.bp4-dark` apply theme variants by inverting colors globally. The `.bp4-dark` class swaps variable-derived colors using CSS specificity.

### Using ThemeProvider

For centralized control, use Blueprint's ThemeProvider wrapper. It injects theme context through the component tree, automatically handling CSS variable updates when the theme prop changes.

### CSS Custom Properties Approach

Blueprint JS uses CSS custom properties for color management. Create a global stylesheet to redefine default variables, affecting all components. This approach maintains design consistency while applying brand-specific colors across your application.(no content)You can replace Blueprint's default colors by reassigning CSS variables at the root level. Components automatically inherit these values, ensuring uniform color application without modifying component source files.

### Dark Theme Style Variables (SCSS)

Dark Theme Style Variables are used to build custom UI components that should look similar to dark-themed BlueprintJS components.

Key dark theme SCSS variables include:
- `$pt-dark-dialog-box-shadow`, `$pt-dark-input-box-shadow`
- `$pt-dark-popover-box-shadow`, `$pt-dark-tooltip-box-shadow`

### Custom Theming with blueprint-styler

Dark theme switching is handled by CSS custom properties now. Blueprint uses global CSS to style its React components, allowing the styles to be overridden or replaced entirely. This project (blueprint-styler) overrides the default Blueprint global CSS with a version that uses `--custom-properties` (also referred to as 'Tokens').

### System Preference Detection

You can detect system preferences using `window.matchMedia`. This useEffect initializes theme based on OS settings and updates when system preference changes.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on my search, here's what I found about Blueprint.js v6 styling best practices and customization in 2025:

## Blueprint.js v6 Overview

Blueprint 6.0 is now available (as of June 2025). It focuses on the removal of deprecated and legacy code and the modernization of the codebase.

### Key Changes in v6

- CSS namespace changed from bp5- to bp6-.
- React 16 and 17 are no longer supported as peer dependencies of Blueprint. Blueprint no longer produces UMD bundles from its packages.
- @blueprintjs/datetime2 is now deprecated and should no longer be used. All component implementations have been merged into @blueprintjs/datetime.

## Styling and Customization Best Practices

### Using CSS Custom Properties
You can create custom global styles for Blueprint.js components using CSS --custom-properties. Blueprint uses global CSS to style its React components, allowing the styles to be overridden or replaced entirely.

### Blueprint-Styler Tool
The blueprint-styler project comments out all .bpX-dark theme declarations, as dark theme switching is handled by CSS custom properties now.(no content)You can override vars and styles by copy-pasting vars from files in ./src/styles/_blueprint/common/ and ./src/styles/_blueprint/components/, and changing the values to override them.

### CSS Overriding Techniques
Override specific component styles using CSS selectors matching Blueprint's BEM class naming convention. Increase specificity by combining class selectors or using !important to bypass default styles.(no content)You can isolate style overrides using CSS Modules to prevent global conflicts. Import generated class names into React components and merge them with Blueprint classes via template literals.

### Why Blueprint Uses Global CSS
Regular CSS is easier for developers to override and theme—this is especially important when authoring a library instead of an application. Blueprint has some use cases where they want to allow consumers to do this after build time, which is cumbersome to do with inline styles.

### Light Theme Variables
Light Theme Style Variables are used to build custom UI components that should look similar to Light-themed BlueprintJS components.

For more details, you can visit the [Blueprint.js documentation](https://blueprintjs.com/docs/) or the [Blueprint 6.0 wiki](https://github.com/palantir/blueprint/wiki/Blueprint-6.0).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Blueprint.js CSS variables with the `$pt-` prefix for dark theme:

## Blueprint.js Dark Theme CSS Variables

Dark Theme Style Variables are used to build custom UI components that should look similar to dark-themed BlueprintJS components.

### Key Dark Theme Variables (`$pt-dark-*`)

The main dark theme style variables include:
- **`$pt-dark-dialog-box-shadow`**: `inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2), 0 2px 4px rgba(17, 20, 24, 0.4), 0 8px 24px rgba(17, 20, 24, 0.4)`
- **`$pt-dark-input-box-shadow`**: `inset 0 0 0 1px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.2), inset 0 -1px 1px 0 #8f99a8`(no content)- **`$pt-dark-tooltip-box-shadow`**: `0 2px 4px rgba(17, 20, 24, 0.4), 0 8px 24px rgba(17, 20, 24, 0.4)`(no content)- **`$pt-dark-popover-border-color`**: `hsl(215deg, 3%, 38%)` - This is tuned to match semi-transparent white borders on a dark theme background, since they can't use `$gray1` because its blue tint is noticeably different from other white borders in dark theme.

### Color Aliases for Dark Theme

Additional dark theme color aliases include:
- **`$pt-dark-app-background-color`**: The dark theme application background color
- **`$pt-dark-code-background-color`**: The dark theme code background color

### How to Use

To use these variables, import the Blueprint variables in your SCSS file:
```scss
@import "@blueprintjs/core/lib/scss/variables";
```

### Enabling Dark Theme

Toggling themes involves applying the `bp4-dark` class to a parent element, which cascades updated CSS variables to child components.(no content)For centralized control, you can use Blueprint's `ThemeProvider` wrapper, which injects theme context through the component tree, automatically handling CSS variable updates when the theme prop changes.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Blueprint.js v6:

## Blueprint.js v6 Overview

Blueprint 6.0 is now available (as of June 2025).(no content)It focuses on the removal of deprecated and legacy code and the modernization of the codebase. The CSS namespace changed from bp5- to bp6-.

### Key Changes in v6

- React 16 and 17 are no longer supported as peer dependencies of Blueprint.
- Blueprint no longer produces UMD bundles from its packages.
- Removed the deprecated Classes.SELECT class name and its associated styles.

### Spacing System

There is a "Spacing System Migration: 10px to 4px" documented in the Blueprint wiki. This indicates a significant change in the spacing system between versions.

### Colors Package

Blueprint is a React UI toolkit for the web. The @blueprintjs/colors package contains color variables for Blueprint's color palette.(no content)The React.js Blueprint provides different types of color schemes that can be used while styling a web application. The core colors are used in user interface design and each color is linked to what we call a visual intent.

**Core Colors include:**
- Blue: Available in 5 different shades (Blue 1 to 5). Its intent is primary.
- Green: Available in 5 different shades (Green 1 to 5). Its intent is success.
- Orange: Available in 5 different shades (Orange 1 to 5). Its intent is warning.
- Red: Available in 5 different shades.

### CSS Architecture

Blueprint colors are used in Sass scripting language which is a preprocessor style sheet language compiled into CSS.

For more detailed documentation on v6 spacing classes and the complete color palette, you may want to visit the official Blueprint documentation at https://blueprintjs.com/docs/ or the GitHub wiki at https://github.com/palantir/blueprint/wiki/Blueprint-6.0.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Blueprint.js v6:

## Blueprint.js v6 Overview

Blueprint 6.0 is now available (as of June 2025).(no content)It focuses on the removal of deprecated and legacy code and the modernization of the codebase. The CSS namespace changed from bp5- to bp6-.

### Key Changes in v6

- React 16 and 17 are no longer supported as peer dependencies of Blueprint.
- Blueprint no longer produces UMD bundles from its packages.
- Removed the deprecated Classes.SELECT class name and its associated styles.

### Spacing System

There is a "Spacing System Migration: 10px to 4px" documented in the Blueprint wiki. This indicates a significant change in the spacing system between versions.

### Colors Package

Blueprint is a React UI toolkit for the web. The @blueprintjs/colors package contains color variables for Blueprint's color palette.(no content)The React.js Blueprint provides different types of color schemes that can be used while styling a web application. The core colors are used in user interface design and each color is linked to what we call a visual intent.

**Core Colors include:**
- Blue: Available in 5 different shades (Blue 1 to 5). Its intent is primary.
- Green: Available in 5 different shades (Green 1 to 5). Its intent is success.
- Orange: Available in 5 different shades (Orange 1 to 5). Its intent is warning.
- Red: Available in 5 different shades.

### CSS Architecture

Blueprint colors are used in Sass scripting language which is a preprocessor style sheet language compiled into CSS.

For more detailed documentation on v6 spacing classes and the complete color palette, you may want to visit the official Blueprint documentation at https://blueprintjs.com/docs/ or the GitHub wiki at https://github.com/palantir/blueprint/wiki/Blueprint-6.0.
